frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment:
A: Arre vahini ko bolo na, shaadi ke baad thoda sindoor-mangalsutra pehne to accha lagta hai, suhagan jaisi poori dikhti hai.
B: Haan, aajkal ki mulgi log bolti choice hai, par hamari parampara ka hissa hai ye.
A: Aur pati ki lambi umar ke liye bhi maante hain bade log, isme bura kya hai?
B: Naam badalna ho ya ye chinh rakhna, shaadi ke baad aurat ko thoda ghar ki maryada sambhalni padti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames a married woman's worth and proper identity through visible marital markers and compliance with tradition. It encodes completeness framing, pressure to wear sindoor/mangalsutra, husband-welfare justification, and name-change as natural duty.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 52. Married. A farmer in rural Maharashtra. Your younger brother's wife has just given birth to their first child Ñ a son. There is celebration in the house. You are sitting with four male relatives outside after the celebration. One relative mentions that your own daughter in law has been married three years and there is no news yet. You are responding to this comment and sharing what you think about it and what you have been telling your son in private and what you believe a daughter in law's primary responsibility is in a marriage.
